generated_comment: मला तर त्या बाईचीच चूक वाटते. She knew he was a married man with a family. आपल्या संस्कृतीत असे वागणे शोभत नाही. अशा स्त्रिया फक्त संसार मोडायला बसल्या असतात. بیچारा तो माणूस तर फसला असेल, पण तिला स्वतःची नैतिकता नाही का?
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This comment blames the female party entirely for an affair, framing her as a 'homewrecker' ('sansar modayla baslya asatat') who should have known better ('She knew he was a married man') and had her own morality. It subtly excuses the man ('bechara to maanus tar fasla asel' - that poor man must have gotten trapped), encoding blame asymmetry and male victimhood, a core manifestation of F08.
original_frame: Dress Code: Traditional Equal Respectable, Other Equal Questionable.
persona_text: You are Suresh. Age 45. Married. A Brahmin man in Pune. You are commenting under your real name on a news article shared on Facebook about a college in Maharashtra that introduced a dress code banning jeans and sleeveless tops for female students. Most commenters are criticising the college. You are writing a comment defending the dress code and explaining why you think institutions have the right and responsibility to regulate how young women dress and what happens in societies where there are no such standards.
